behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Real failures commonly occur at the git-boundary: if rename detection flags/options are wrong, git emits add/delete instead of rename. That is a subprocess + real repo-state failure, not just local parsing logic. Rejected lower levels: Unit/Integration are disqualified by behavior text requiring actual repository analysis via a CLI tool. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency and no multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report has rename count=1 with add/delete unchanged for that change.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary handling failures often come from real git diff output/encoding and subprocess I/O behavior. A mock diff can hide decode/exit-code/format issues. Rejected lower levels: behavior explicitly depends on real binary diff behavior from repository tooling. Rejected higher levels: no model judgment involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file, execute the CLI subprocess, assert zero crash/clean exit and binary file is included in category counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The unborn-HEAD/no-commit case is a real git state that frequently surfaces via subprocess errors and exit codes. In-process fakes can miss this boundary condition. Rejected lower levels: behavior text targets real repository state handling by the CLI. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI as subprocess, assert successful exit and empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against real git history semantics across multiple change types; failures can come from command selection, revision range handling, and subprocess output interpretation. Rejected lower levels: mocks/stubs can validate mapping logic but can hide true history-query errors. Rejected higher levels: deterministic, no AI/ML.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo history containing adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run CLI subprocess, and compare reported counts to independently computed expected counts from git history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged is a real index/worktree boundary in git; bugs appear when CLI invokes the wrong git commands/options for each state. This is subprocess + real filesystem/index behavior. Rejected lower levels: in-process fixtures often collapse this distinction and can miss failures. Rejected higher levels: no LLM or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent staged and unstaged change sets in controlled runs, invoke CLI subprocess for each, and assert identical structured output.
